2606.29793.title: Fund2Persona: A Framework for Building and Refining Financial Advisor Personas from Fund Disclosure Data
2606.29793.paper_summary: Demand for personalized financial advising is growing, but consistent advisor expertise is difficult to obtain, scale, and encode in LLM systems. Simple persona prompts rarely specify how a financial advisor should reason and often drift toward generic recommendations. We propose Fund2Persona, a framework that grounds financial-advisor personas in fund disclosures, holdings transitions, market context, and manager commentary, then refines them through an agentic actor--scorer--patcher loop. We evaluate the resulting personas on held-out holdings-transition reconstruction and manager-commentary alignment, where they better recover portfolio decisions and grounded manager interpretation than generic baselines. We further study two downstream diagnostics: market-scenario generation, where persona retrieval broadens plausible investment views beyond repeated generic rollouts, and advisory dialogues grounded in investor profiles, where matched personas give more specific and useful advice than a generic advisor. These results suggest that fund-data-grounded financial-advisor personas can make manager-specific investment expertise portable rather than merely changing an LLM's surface style.
2606.29793.paper_summary_zh: 個性化財務顧問的需求正在增長，但持續的顧問專業知識難以獲得、擴展和在大規模語言模型系統中編碼。簡單的人物提示很少具體說明財務顧問應該如何推理，並且往往偏向於通用建議。我們提出了 Fund2Persona，一個將財務顧問人物角色根植於基金披露、持有轉換、市場背景和經理評論的框架，然後通過一個代理行為者--評分者--修補者的循環來精煉它們。我們在保留的持有轉換重建和經理評論對齊上評估了結果人物角色，在這些方面，它們比通用基準更好地恢復了投資組合決策和根植的經理解釋。我們進一步研究了兩個下游診斷：市場情境生成，其中人物檢索擴大了合理的投資觀點，超越了重複的通用推出，以及基於投資者檔案的顧問對話，其中匹配的人物角色提供的建議比通用顧問更具體和有用。這些結果表明，基於基金數據的財務顧問人物角色可以使經理特定的投資專業知識變得可攜帶，而不僅僅是改變大規模語言模型的表面風格。
2606.29793.author: Suhwan Park et.al.
2606.29793.authors: Suhwan Park, Hoyoung Lee, Zhangyang Wang, Alejandro Lopez-Lira, Young Cha, Chanyeol Choi, Jaewon Choi, Yongjae Lee
